turn1.user: Comparative study — not a decision doc. I want a side-by-side study of how three secrets management approaches handle rotation at scale: HashiCorp Vault with dynamic credentials, AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation lambdas, and a manual rotation workflow with encrypted parameter store. Compare them across rotation latency, audit trail completeness, blast radius on credential compromise, and complexity of cross-account access patterns. This is a comparative study for my own understanding — do not produce a migration guide, do not write IAM policies, do not declare a winner.
